title: berula repanda
intro: Berula repanda, also known as Indian pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Berula repanda include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce stress and anxiety, and act as an anticonvulsant. Berula repanda is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
